summary: First iteration end-to-end testing of SCOUT (mobile PWA). Backend API 25/26 tests passing (96%); frontend flows for login, register, dashboard, spots, calendar, event detail, booking, settings, profile, scout-black all work. CRITICAL bug: Map screen is completely broken (uncaught runtime error 'Map container is already initialized') due to React.StrictMode + react-leaflet@4.2.1 incompatibility — full white/blank UI when navigating to /map.

minor.issue: Returns 500 instead of 400 when called with neither X-Session-ID header nor JSON body. `await request.json()` raises on empty body. Wrap in try/except or short-circuit when content-length is 0.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Page crashes with uncaught runtime error 'Map container is already initialized.' coming from react-leaflet inside React.StrictMode. The MapContainer is mounted/unmounted twice in dev mode (StrictMode double-invocation) and react-leaflet 4.2.1 cannot tear down between renders. Result: /map renders a completely blank black screen and the dev error overlay appears in development. Reproducible on hard refresh AND on bottom-nav navigation. Affects all marker rendering and the entire Map feature.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): Map page broken. Options: (a) Remove <React.StrictMode> wrapper from /app/frontend/src/index.js (simplest, common fix in CRA + react-leaflet stacks); (b) Upgrade react-leaflet (4.2.1 has the StrictMode dev bug — versions >=4.2.1 still affected, consider 5.x with React 18+); (c) Wrap MapContainer in a key that only assigns once per mount and ensure cleanup with `whenCreated`/`map.remove()`. Recommended fix: remove StrictMode for production parity since the rest of the app does not require it.

FIX (MINOR): /api/auth/google/session — return 400 instead of 500 when both X-Session-ID header and JSON body are absent. Wrap `await request.json()` in try/except or check `request.headers.get('content-length')`.

OPTIONAL: Calendar opens on October 2024 (events default month) — works, but consider showing today's month and an info banner '0 eventos' if user lands in Jan 2026 with no events; otherwise confusing UX once October 2024 is in the past.
critical_code_review_comments: MapScreen.jsx: react-leaflet StrictMode bug not handled. Production build may also exhibit subtle re-init issues if any parent re-mounts MapContainer.
server.py: google_session endpoint reads request body unconditionally; raises on empty body. Should validate content-length before parsing.
server.py: /api/auth/google/session inserts a new user_sessions record on every login without dedup, leading to row growth. Consider upsert by session_token or user_id.
server.py: register/login set Secure SameSite=None cookies — correct for cross-site preview URL. Verified cookies set & read across requests.

BottomNav data-testids are nav-home/nav-spots/nav-map/nav-events/nav-profile (not bottom-nav-* as some prompts assume). All 5 tabs render and route correctly. Profile reflects user (Arthur, 1250 pts, BLACK).
seed_data_creation: None — seeded data already created on backend startup (6 spots, 6 events, 8 partnerships, 1 admin user). Test users created during tests have prefix 'test_/booker_/sub_/notif_' and live in MongoDB; can be cleaned with prefix later if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_scout_api.py is the regression baseline (run via `pytest backend/tests/test_scout_api.py -v`). Critical frontend blocker is the Map page (/map) — verify after main agent's fix by visiting https://google-stitch-app.preview.emergentagent.com/map and confirming .leaflet-container renders with markers and no 'Map container is already initialized' page error. Also re-run the google_session 400 minor fix.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause of Map crash: index.js wraps <App/> in <React.StrictMode>. In dev, StrictMode mounts → unmounts → remounts MapContainer. react-leaflet 4.2.1 keeps a reference to the underlying Leaflet map on the same DOM node, so the second mount throws 'Map container is already initialized.' which propagates and triggers React error boundary / red-screen overlay, leaving the page blank. Reproduction: log in → click bottom nav 'Mapa' OR open /map directly. Mitigation: drop StrictMode (quickest), or upgrade react-leaflet, or force-remove the leaflet instance on unmount with a custom hook.
